key_themes: Record $190B backlog and surging orders providing multi-year revenue visibility
Strong historical earnings beats with 2026 EPS guidance of $7.10–$7.40
$1B+ investments in U.S. manufacturing, defense production, and 5,000 new jobs
Overwhelmingly bullish Wall Street consensus (Strong Buy, ~$340–$350 avg price target)
Short-term profit-taking and de-risking ahead of April 21, 2026 Q1 earnings
sources: 12+ recent web news articles (Zacks, Yahoo Finance, Fool, GE press releases, MarketBeat)
20+ X posts analyzed (semantic + keyword search since Jan 2026)
Analyst ratings and targets from 12–20 firms (average PT $340–$352)
summary: GE Aerospace (the core business behind the GE ticker post-spin-offs) enjoys strongly positive investor and analyst sentiment driven by aerospace tailwinds. A record ~$190 billion backlog, 74% YoY order growth, consistent earnings beats, and major U.S. manufacturing investments signal robust long-term demand in commercial engines and defense. While the stock saw a ~5% pullback in mid-April 2026 on pre-earnings profit-taking and valuation resets, analysts remain overwhelmingly bullish with Strong Buy ratings and price targets implying 10–15%+ upside from current ~$300 levels.
